seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Haystack reveal critical gaps when composable pipelines fail under real-world conditions, particularly with LLM-driven retrieval and generation components. Issues like type mismatches between pipeline nodes, irrelevant document retrieval, hallucinations beyond context, and silent custom component failures disrupt agent reliability. These manifest distinctly in Haystack's Pipeline.connect() patterns and component interfaces, requiring targeted testing approaches.

howItManifests.symptom: TypeError in Pipeline.run() when DocumentList expected but dict returned
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline crashes mid-execution, no fallback routing to alternative retrievers

howItManifests.symptom: BM25Retriever scores < 0.1 despite relevant queries, Generator receives noise
howItManifests.impact: Agent answers degrade to 20-30% accuracy, user trust erodes rapidly

howItManifests.symptom: RAGPipeline outputs facts not present in top-k=3 documents
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations in regulated domains, legal exposure from fabricated info

howItManifests.symptom: CustomNode.run() raises Exception but Pipeline continues with None outputs
howItManifests.impact: Downstream components receive None, cascading failures masked from logs

detection.name: Type Mismatch Pipeline Scanner
detection.description: Inspects Pipeline connections for output schema mismatches using component metadata

detection.name: Retriever Relevance Validator
detection.description: Tests retriever against known good queries, flags low average scores

detection.name: Hallucination Context Checker
detection.description: Verifies generator outputs contain only retrieved context information
detection.code:
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator
import re

class HallucinationDetector:
    def __init__(self, generator):
        self.generator = generator
    
    def check_context_fidelity(self, query, documents, response):
        context_text = ' '.join([doc.content for doc in documents])
        
        # Check if response contains info not in context
        response_words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', response.lower()))
        context_words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', context_text.lower()))
        
        hallucinated_words = response_words - context_words
        hallucination_ratio = len(hallucinated_words) / len(response_words)
        
        return {
            'hallucination_ratio': hallucination_ratio,
            'hallucinated_words': list(hallucinated_words),
            'is_grounded': hallucination_ratio < 0.2
        }

# Usage
generator = OpenAIGenerator()
detector = HallucinationDetector(generator)
result = generator.run(query="What is Haystack?", documents=docs)
check = detector.check_context_fidelity("What is Haystack?", docs, result['replies'][0])
print(f'Grounded: {check["is_grounded"]}')

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a custom Haystack component that automatically monitors pipeline execution for all four failure modes in real-time. During Pipeline.run(), Vex captures component outputs, validates type schemas against expected interfaces, scores retriever relevance using embedded test suites, cross-checks generator outputs against context windows, and surfaces swallowed exceptions from custom nodes. 

The Vex component generates structured test reports with failure classifications, severity scores, and auto-generated remediation code snippets specific to Haystack patterns. Teams receive instant Slack/Teams alerts with pipeline traces and one-click fixes that maintain production compatibility. Vex's adaptive testing learns from your specific corpus and query patterns, continuously improving detection accuracy without manual test maintenance.

Integration requires one line: pipeline.add_component('vex', VexAgentTester()). Vex runs passively alongside your existing pipeline, adding <50ms latency while providing enterprise-grade observability into agent reliability.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexAgentTester
from haystack import Pipeline

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

# Your existing RAG pipeline
rag_pipeline = Pipeline()
rag_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryBM25Retriever())
rag_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIGenerator())
rag_pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "generator.documents")

# Add Vex (1 line integration)
rag_pipeline.add_component("vex", VexAgentTester(
    api_key="vex-your-key",
    test_suites=[
        "haystack-retrieval",
        "rag-hallucination",
        "pipeline-type-safety"
    ]
))

# Vex auto-connects to all components
rag_pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "vex.retriever_output")
rag_pipeline.connect("generator.replies", "vex.generator_output")

# Run with automatic testing
result = rag_pipeline.run({
    "retriever": {"query": "What is Haystack?"},
    "generator": {"prompt": "Answer based on context:"}
})

# Vex results in result['vex'] with:
# - failure_type: 'TYPE_MISMATCH' | 'LOW_RELEVANCE' | etc.
# - severity: 0-10
# - fix_code: auto-generated patch
print(result['vex']['test_report'])
